title: The Whispering Cave
description: Entering the Whispering Cave. The honorable skeleton, designed monsters, and deepening mystery.

lines: The cave had earned its name honestly. It whispered.
Not words, exactly. More like the memory of words — the sound a sentence makes after everyone has forgotten what it meant.
The air tasted of iron and wet limestone, and something older beneath that. Shadows moved before they did.

steps.text: The monsters here were different from the road fodder. Sharper. More deliberate. As if performing the role of 'cave monster' rather than simply being one.
The antechamber held a knight in ancient plate — still at parade rest, waiting. The party moved past. It was the Fighter's duel to answer, when the moment came.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Someone made this harder. Someone went through the cave and... arranged it. Court magic. The formal kind. The kind with a wax seal.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Someone in the capital designed this floor?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Someone in the capital designed this floor.

steps.text: The deepest chamber was where the whispers converged.
